channel.title: Machine Learning on Frank Huang
channel.description: Recent content in Machine Learning on Frank Huang

item.title: How to improve your neural net -- note of deeplearning.ai from coursera
item.description: Regularization Problems what is high bias and high variance? High bias: meaning training data performance problems bigger neural net train longer different architect High variance: meaning dev set performance problems aka overfitting need more data: develop the train / dev / test set. regularization: weight decay in order to prevent overfitting, one should keep the weight low Regularization in Logistic regression Regularization in Neural Net L1 regu L2 regu

item.title: Multilayer Neural network -- note of deeplearning.ai from coursera
item.description: Lost function for logistic classification: $$ J=-\frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=0}^{m}\left(y^{(i)} \log \left(a^{[2](i)}\right)+\left(1-y^{(i)}\right) \log \left(1-a^{[2](i)}\right)\right) $$ Several questions to take away: forward phase: 1. what to cache in layer l? backprop phase: 1. what derivative value should be calculated for layer l? 2. what will be the output for layer l? 3. what will the input for layer l? 4. why is the update parameter work by subtracting the derivative? that is $$ \boldsymbol{W}^{[I]}=\boldsymbol{W}^{[I]}-\alpha d \boldsymbol{W}^{[I]} $$ 5.

item.title: My thougts about Linear Regression
item.description: Suppose we have a for variables that are correlated lasso regression ridge regression spark-submit kept showing: yarn.Client: Application report for application (state: ACCEPTED) spark 参数配置 –conf -> shell parameter -> conf/spark-defaults.conf - num-executors - executor-cores - spark.default.parallelism - total-executor-cores memory setting: memory is splitted into 3 parts: for tasks(20%), for shuffle(20%), for storage(60%) - spark.storage.memoryFraction: cache/persist operation - spark.shuffle.memoryFraction: memory for shuffle manipulation - executor-memory: total memory for each executor - driver-memory

item.title: Support vector machine
item.description: v is the point to be classified. w is a vector points to the hyperplane perpendicularly. $$hyperplane:y=w\cdot \xrightarrow { V } +b$$ There are 2 classes which are black and red respectively. if w v + b > 0, then the v belongs to black if w v + b < 0, then the v belongs to red if w * v + v = 0, then the v is on the decision boundary which is also the hyperplane.
